subject: Chemistry
summary: Biomolecules are a type of organic compound found in living organisms. They are made up of carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, and nitrogen atoms. The properties and functions of biomolecules vary depending on their structure and composition. Proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, nucleic acids, and vitamins are all examples of biomolecules. Biomolecules play crucial roles in many biological processes, such as cell signaling, metabolism, and DNA replication. Understanding the structures and functions of biomolecules is essential for understanding biological systems and developing treatments for diseases. In this summary, we will cover the key concepts and formulas related to biomolecules, common mistakes students make on exams, and tips for answering questions about biomolecules. We will also discuss how biomolecules are examined in JEE Mains and NEET UG exams.

questions.question: What is the main function of carbohydrates?

options.A: Energy source
options.B: Structural component
options.C: Regulator of gene expression
options.D: Transport molecule

questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are an energy source for cells
